title: Glyceria capillaris
intro: Glyceria capillaris, also known as manna grass or sweet grass, is a type of perennial grass belonging to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Glyceria capillaris include glycosides, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Glyceria capillaris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to promote a sense of calm and well-being.
taxonomy: Glyceria capillaris, commonly known as hair grass or wind grass, is a type of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Poaceae, which includes other types of grasses. Glyceria capillaris is further classified as a genus within the tribe Meliceae. Within this tribe, it is a member of the subfamily Alodaeoideae. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics.
uses: Glyceria capillaris, also known as hair grass, is a plant traditionally used in medicine. Its medicinal uses include treating digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion.
